summary: Backend testing for WaSenderAPI integration and phone update features - all 23 tests passed (100%). Verified: GET /api/whatsapp/settings returns correct structure (id, is_active, has_pat, wasender_session_id, connected), GET /api/whatsapp/status returns 'not_configured' when no PAT, POST /api/whatsapp/setup validates API key and rejects invalid with 400, PUT /api/whatsapp/settings updates business_phone and is_active, PUT /api/auth/update-phone saves phone and rejects invalid (<8 chars), GET /api/auth/me returns user with phone field, all endpoints require authentication.
context_for_next_testing_agent: WaSenderAPI integration endpoints are all working correctly. All authentication is enforced. External WaSenderAPI calls will fail with invalid API keys (expected behavior - no real API key in test env). Phone update feature works with validation (rejects <8 chars). Phone is persisted in User table and returned by /api/auth/me. WhatsApp settings use wasender_pat, wasender_session_id, wasender_session_key fields in WhatsAppSettings model.

tested_features.GET /api/whatsapp/settings: PASS - Returns {id, is_active, has_pat, wasender_session_id, connected}
tested_features.GET /api/whatsapp/status: PASS - Returns 'not_configured' when no PAT set
tested_features.POST /api/whatsapp/setup: PASS - Validates API key, rejects invalid with 400
tested_features.POST /api/whatsapp/setup_validation: PASS - Returns 422 for missing api_key field
tested_features.PUT /api/whatsapp/settings: PASS - Updates business_phone and is_active fields
tested_features.PUT /api/auth/update-phone: PASS - Saves phone number to user profile
tested_features.GET /api/auth/me_phone: PASS - Returns user with phone field
tested_features.POST /api/whatsapp/connect: PASS - Requires session configured first (400 without)
tested_features.GET /api/whatsapp/qrcode: PASS - Returns error when no session configured
tested_features.POST /api/whatsapp/test: PASS - Rejects when WhatsApp not connected

mocked_apis_note: WaSenderAPI is a real external service. External API calls fail with invalid keys (expected). All internal endpoints work correctly with proper error handling.
